description_long: This is a double-headed battle axe with a robust wooden handle and two large, symmetrical black blades. The handle is cylindrical, featuring a textured grip at the lower end, likely wrapped for improved handling. The blades are wide, crescent-shaped, and attached securely to the handle with visible metal fastenings and decorative bindings. The central mounting area where the blades meet the handle is reinforced, possibly with additional metal or leather strapping. The overall design is both functional and ornamental, suggesting it could serve as a display piece or a functional tool. The color palette is dominated by dark metal and rich brown wood, with subtle accents from the bindings.
description: A double-headed battle axe with a wooden handle, black crescent blades, and decorative bindings. The design is robust and visually striking.
description_view_0: Front view showing both wide, symmetrical black blades and the full length of the wooden handle.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile of the blades and the cylindrical handle, with the blade edges barely visible.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying the double blades and handle, with fastenings and bindings visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the thin blade profile and the handle's grip texture.
attribute_handle_type: Straight cylindrical wooden handle with a wrapped grip section, slightly contoured for ergonomic holding.
attribute_blade_design: Double-headed, crescent-shaped black blades with symmetrical edges and reinforced central mounting.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged and strapped mounting with visible fastenings and decorative bindings.
